findings.title: Zero-value `transferFrom` lets anyone tamper with another user's pressure accounting and force release/halving

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can use zero-value `transferFrom` to tamper with another user's pressure accounting and force settlement

findings.title: Pressure settlement uses manipulable spot pair balance and total supply instead of sale-time state

best_agent_match.title: Burn and Steam payout depend on manipulable spot pair balances rather than fixed sale-time data

findings.title: Sell transfers emit a `Transfer` value that does not match actual balance changes

best_agent_match.title: Pair-sale transfers emit a falsified `Transfer` amount that does not match balance changes

rejected_candidates.title: Unauthorized Steam Token Minting via Owner Privileges
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive: `Steam` is deployed via `new Steam(...)` inside `UpSwing`, so `Steam._UPS` is the `UpSwing` contract address, not the external deployer.

rejected_candidates.title: Centralized Admin Control with No Timelock
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a governance/trust assumption, not a distinct code vulnerability in the permissionless protocol logic.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero Address Validation in setUNIv2
rejected_candidates.reason: Requires privileged misconfiguration by an allowed address; it does not create an independent permissionless exploit beyond trusted-admin control.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by Zero in amountPressure
rejected_candidates.reason: Only reachable if all UPS supply is voluntarily burned away; that is a terminal self-destruction scenario rather than a realistic independent protocol bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy Vulnerability in _transfer
rejected_candidates.reason: The only external call is `sync()` on the configured pair; exploiting reentrancy would require a privileged actor to point `UNIv2` at a malicious contract, so this is not an independent permissionless issue.

rejected_candidates.title: Integer Overflow in amountPressure with High Leverage
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive: arithmetic uses `SafeMath`, and `leverage` is a `uint8`, so the proposed >1000 overflow path is not valid.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Event Emissions for Critical Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Transparency issue only; not a realistic protocol-security vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Leverage Can Be Set to Zero Breaking Protocol
rejected_candidates.reason: Relies entirely on privileged admin action and is covered by the protocol's trusted-admin model rather than an exploitable bug.
